hooks.command: >&2 echo "[hook] git-reminder"; INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command'); FIRST=$(echo "$CMD" | awk '{ for (i=1;i<=NF;i++) if ($i !~ /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*=/) { print $i; exit } }'); [ "$FIRST" = "git" ] || exit 0; jq -n '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"PreToolUse",additionalContext:"Reminder: use `rtk git ...` instead of plain `git ...`. rtk wraps diff, log, status, show, add, commit, push, pull, branch, fetch, stash, and worktree with compact output."}}'

>&2 echo "[hook] commit-hygiene"; INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command'); echo "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[[:space:]])(rtk[[:space:]]+)?git[[:space:]]+commit([[:space:]]|$)' || exit 0; SESSION=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // "default"'); SENTINEL="/tmp/claude-commit-hygiene-$SESSION"; [ -f "$SENTINEL" ] && exit 0; touch "$SENTINEL"; jq -n '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"PreToolUse",additionalContext:"Commit message: imperative subject. Body covers what changed and WHY, including non-obvious context the diff does not show. Do not restate what the diff already shows. Include the Co-Authored-By trailer on agent-authored commits. This reminder fires once per session."}}'

>&2 echo "[hook] karpathy"; jq -n '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"SessionStart",additionalContext:"Karpathy Guidelines: for any planning work this session, hold the plan to these five principles.

1. Surface, do not assume. State assumptions in the plan. Name multiple interpretations instead of silently picking one. If unclear, ask before ExitPlanMode.

2. Minimum viable change. Plan only what the task requires. No speculative features, unused abstractions, extra sections, or error handling for impossible cases. If the plan could be half the size, shrink it.

3. Surgical edits. Touch only what the task demands. No adjacent improvements, reformatting, unrelated rewrites, or style cleanup. Match existing conventions. Remove only the orphans your changes create.

4. Verifiable success. The plan must say how you will know it is done. Code: test passes, command exits zero, output appears. Text: named claims present, structure intact, reviewable in one pass.

5. Isolate reverts. Split unrelated concerns across separate commits so any one can be reverted without dragging the others. Do not bundle a bug fix with a refactor, a rename with a feature, or a dependency bump with logic changes. If the plan produces a single commit covering multiple concerns, split it in the plan before coding. Modularity here means separable commits, not speculative abstractions (principles 2 and 3 still apply).

When you call ExitPlanMode, state explicitly that you checked the plan against all five karpathy principles and summarize any revisions you made (or confirm none were needed)."}}'
